mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 5.5 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving immediate equity risk premium compression and positive short-term market returns.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative or flat 5-day forward returns in more than 60 percent of triggered instances over a 5-year backtest period.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in industrial production and housing starts signals synchronized real-economy expansion, which validates corporate profit growth expectations and lifts equity valuations over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by at least 150 basis points over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery phases, rising core inflation combined with stable short-term rates signals successful demand normalization, which expands equity earnings forecasts and drives positive quarterly market returns.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns turn negative when CPIAUCSL rises above 2.5 percent while FEDFUNDS remains below 2.0 percent for consecutive quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth directly increases corporate revenue trajectories, which reduces equity risk premiums and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: The rolling 12-month Spearman correlation between quarterly GDP growth surprises and 5-day forward returns falls below 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in industrial production exceeding 0.5 percent confirms supply-side capacity utilization, which reduces input cost volatility and supports positive equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The indicator produces a negative predictive rank correlation with 20-day forward returns for three consecutive calendar years.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and rising consumer credit balances indicate synchronized income and spending expansion, which sustains corporate cash flow growth and drives positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields a negative information coefficient over any 5-year out-of-sample test period.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansion environments, rapid housing permit growth combined with accelerating consumer price inflation signals overheating demand, which triggers monetary tightening expectations and generates negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive 5-day forward returns in more than 55 percent of triggered instances over a 10-year walk-forward test.
